findings.reachability: Reachable by any unauthenticated user via wp_ajax_nopriv_prdctfltr_respond_550 (line 38); no authentication or capability check protects this handler.
findings.justification: The search phrase is taken directly from $_POST['pf_filters'][$_POST['pf_id']]['s'] (line 65) with no sanitization (no sanitize_text_field, wp_unslash, or strip_tags) before being passed to Helpers::searchProducts($phrase); safety depends entirely on downstream sanitization inside the search engine.

detection_pattern.section_name: Missing Input Sanitization: Raw $_POST nested key passed to search
detection_pattern.description: Detects places where a nested $_POST value used as a search phrase is not sanitized before use. Without upstream sanitization, security relies entirely on downstream callers — removing or changing those callers introduces injection risk.

findings.reachability: Reachable by any visitor (authenticated or not) via wp_ajax_nopriv_prdctfltr_respond_550 / wp_ajax_prdctfltr_respond_550 (lines 38-39); PHPCS nonce-check suppression comment at line 56 confirms this is intentional.
findings.justification: The AJAX handler performs a server-side product search and populates $this->post_ids which then influences WP_Query results; there is no nonce verification (phpcs:disable WordPress.Security.NonceVerification.Missing at line 56), making it trivially CSRF-able to force a mis-populated search result list in a victim's session.

detection_pattern.section_name: CSRF: wp_ajax_nopriv_ handler without nonce verification
detection_pattern.description: Detects nopriv AJAX handlers that perform state-affecting operations without any nonce check. Without a nonce, any site can craft a cross-origin POST that is executed in the victim's browser context.

findings.reachability: Accessible to all unauthenticated users via wp_ajax_nopriv_prdctfltr_respond_550 (line 38).
findings.justification: The handler fires a backend product search (Helpers::searchProducts) triggered by anonymous visitors with no capability check (no current_user_can call anywhere in the method); while the operation itself is read-only, the lack of any access gate means unauthenticated actors can drive the plugin's internal search engine, potentially leaking information about non-public products if the search engine does not filter by post status.

detection_pattern.section_name: Access Control: nopriv AJAX handler with no capability check
detection_pattern.description: Finds wp_ajax_nopriv_ handlers that perform backend work without verifying the caller has appropriate permissions. Even read-only operations on private data require at least an authentication gate.
